counting_justification:
Study type: primary case series of 235 individuals evaluated for malignant hyperthermia (MH) (Document, Materials and Methods section).
Variant identification: The CACNA1S variant of interest appears explicitly in Table 2B as “c.520C > T / R174W”. Variants listed in this table were detected through the authors’ exon screening (primary data). Variant aliases constructed include cDNA (c.520C>T), protein (p.Arg174Trp, R174W), and the shorthand used in the paper. (Document, Table 2B).
Phenotype mapping: Allowed phenotype = “Malignant hyperthermia” (PHENOTYPE_DICT; GUIDE Table 3 Note 2). The paper reports that among 21 individuals with VUS, 19 were MH-susceptible (MHS) and 2 were MH-negative (MHN) (Document, Results: “Variants of unknown significance were subdivided into 19 malignant-hyperthermia-susceptible individuals and 2 non-malignant-hyperthermia-susceptible individuals.”). However, the authors do not link each specific VUS to its corresponding IVCT phenotype.
Counting decision: Because the carrier(s) of c.520C>T cannot be definitively assigned to the affected (MHS) or unaffected (MHN) subgroup, the case cannot be confirmed as PS4-eligible. Following the counting rules, a, b, c, d are left at 0 and the ambiguity is documented in non_ps4_summary.
Independence & duplication: Only one instance of the variant is reported in this publication; independence cannot be assessed but is moot because the case is not counted.
Estimation policy: Not applied—no confirmed affected carrier count.
Therefore total_ps4_case_counts = 0 for this study.

counting_justification: The study is a primary case series of Norwegian FH probands explicitly stated to be unrelated (Document, Materials and methods, 'The FH probands were unrelated'). Table 4 reports the number of probands per mutation; the row 'L380V' lists 5 probands and 14 total heterozygotes. ps4_mode_citation: ClinGen Cardiomyopathy Expert Panel Specifications to the ACMG/AMP Variant Interpretation Guidelines for MYBPC3 Version 1.0.0 – PS4 sections (case-control framework)
ps4_thresholds_applied: Strong: lower bound 95% CI OR ≥ 20; Moderate: ≥ 10; Supporting: ≥ 5 (Rules for MYBPC3, PS4 sections)
counting_justification:
Variant identification: The paper explicitly states "C2373dupG" in MYBPC3 as a Dutch founder mutation (Table 1; text line: “majority … had one of the Dutch founder mutations … C2373dupG”). This is the same molecular event as NM_000256.3:c.2373dup (p.Trp792Valfs*41).

Study type: primary case series including 16 unrelated index HCM families plus their relatives (Mutation carriers) and 25 normal controls. Therefore it qualifies as primary data (not secondary-only).

Phenotype mapping: Mut+/Phen+ individuals fulfil an HCM clinical diagnosis (ALLOWED_PHENOTYPES). Mut+/Phen− do not express HCM, so they are ‘unaffected’. Controls are healthy volunteers.

Independence & counting: Because all Mutation-carrier relatives come from only 16 families and relatedness is not specified per individual, the default ACMG/ClinGen rule is to count one independent case per family. The text confirms at least one index patient (unrelated) with the C2373dupG variant and manifest HCM; thus a = 1 (minimum, conservative).

Affected variant-negative individuals exist (other mutations) giving b ≥ 1; Table 1 shows 15/46 Mut+/Phen+ lacked C2373dupG. To avoid over-counting relatedness we conservatively use b = 1 (one independent affected without the variant).

Unaffected variant-positive carriers (Mut+/Phen−) are related family members, so independence is uncertain; conservatively not counted (c = 0). No genotype data given for healthy controls, so d = 0.

Estimation policy applied: minimum-case assumption per guideline when independence is unclear.

These choices yield a = 1, b = 1, c = 0, d = 0 and total_ps4_case_counts = 1.

Document reports 46 phenotype-positive carriers, 47 phenotype-negative carriers, but only 16 families. Following the default rule of one case per family unless independence explicit, I conservatively counted one independent affected carrier (a=1). To populate b, at least one affected case lacked the variant, therefore b=1. Unaffected variant-positive relatives likely overlap families, so excluded (c=0). No verified variant data for controls (d=0). Self-critique confirmed adherence to counting-only mandate, variant alias completeness, and conservative deduplication; nonetheless flagged for curator review because the exact number of independent families carrying C2373dupG could be higher.
